description: Use realistic developer-facing UI shells such as service graphs, logs, dashboards, or architecture canvases as the primary demo surface for technical products.
evidence_notes: Railway repeatedly markets through service canvases, logs, and operational panels.
VoidZero uses chart and tool-switching surfaces to express tooling breadth and credibility.
rules: Show operational objects the audience recognizes immediately.
Use one shell language repeatedly so demos feel systemized.
Prioritize legibility of key labels and states over screenshot density.
implementation_notes: Build reusable app-shell wrappers around screenshots, mocked panels, or controlled client-side state.
Use simplified data density on mobile rather than shrinking full desktop canvases blindly.
Expose semantics and alt text for supporting labels outside purely decorative demo surfaces when possible.
avoid_when: The product audience is not tool- or dashboard-native.
The team cannot maintain believable data and states in the demo shell.
The demo becomes visually noisy and obscures the product story.
